backend/main.mo: Main backend actor implementing user profiles, wallet/coin balances, deposit requests, tournament join requests, and admin operations with authorization and blob storage mixins.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting implemented features including user authentication, wallet system, admin deposit verification, and tournament management with associated files and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Comprehensive summaries of all frontend components, pages, and hooks describing the Free Fire gaming-themed UI with authentication, wallet, tournament, and admin functionality.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark themes, defining color schemes and design tokens for the application.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React application with title 'FF PAYALUGA' and root div mounting point.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root application component configuring TanStack Router with six routes (Home, Login, Dashboard, Wallet, Tournament, Admin), Layout wrapper, ProfileSetupModal, and dark theme provider.
frontend/src/components/admin/AccessDeni: Access denied screen component for non-admin users attempting to access the admin panel with navigation back to dashboard.
frontend/src/components/admin/DepositVer: Admin component displaying pending deposit requests in a table with approve and reject actions, showing user ID, amount, and timestamp.
frontend/src/components/admin/JoinReques: Displays a searchable table of tournament join requests with user ID, Free Fire UID, WhatsApp number, entry fee, and join timestamp. Fetches data using useGetAllTournamentJoinRequests hook and supports filtering by search term.
frontend/src/components/admin/Tournament: Admin component for managing tournament configuration including toggling tournament status (open/closed), updating entry fee, match count, and match time. Displays current settings and provides input forms for updates.
frontend/src/components/admin/UserManage: Displays a searchable table of all registered users showing user ID, name, and email. Fetches data using useGetAllUsers hook and supports filtering by search term.
frontend/src/components/common/GamingBut: Reusable button component with gaming-themed styling supporting three variants (primary, secondary, danger) and three sizes (sm, md, lg). Features gradient backgrounds, hover effects, and active state animations.
frontend/src/components/common/GamingCar: Reusable card container component with gaming-themed styling, backdrop blur, and optional glow effects in orange, cyan, or none. Provides consistent card styling across the application.
frontend/src/components/layout/Layout.ts: Main layout wrapper component that includes navigation header, main content area, and footer with copyright and caffeine.ai attribution. Applies gaming texture background to the entire page.
frontend/src/components/layout/Navigatio: Navigation bar component with responsive design (desktop and mobile menus) featuring links to home, dashboard, wallet, tournament, and admin pages. Handles Internet Identity authentication with login/logout functionality.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModa: Modal dialog for first-time user profile setup collecting name and email with Free Fire orange-themed styling and form validation.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and manages the backend actor instance based on Internet Identity authentication state. Handles actor initialization with admin token and invalidates dependent queries when identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides React context and hooks for Internet Identity authentication, managing login state, identity persistence, and AuthClient lifecycle. Exports useInternetIdentity hook and InternetIdentityProvider component for authentication management.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for user profile, balance, deposits, tournaments, and admin operations, integrating with the backend actor and providing toast notifications for mutations.
frontend/src/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for a dark gaming theme, Free Fire brand colors (orange/cyan), and utility classes for gaming textures and color schemes.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query, Internet Identity provider, and renders the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/AdminPanel.tsx: Admin dashboard page with tabs for managing deposits, users, tournament configuration, and join requests; includes authentication and admin role verification.
frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: User dashboard displaying profile info, wallet balance, and tournament status with navigation to wallet and tournament pages; requires authentication.
frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx: Landing page with hero banner, feature cards highlighting tournaments, deposits, quick matches, and security; adapts CTAs based on authentication status.
frontend/src/pages/LoginRegister.tsx: Login page using Internet Identity authentication with automatic registration for new users; redirects authenticated users to dashboard.
frontend/src/pages/TournamentPage.tsx: Tournament page component that displays tournament status, entry fee, user balance, and a join form requiring Free Fire UID and WhatsApp number. Redirects unauthenticated users to login and shows insufficient balance alerts.
frontend/src/pages/WalletDeposit.tsx: Wallet deposit page component that displays user coin balance and a deposit form accepting amounts between ₹10-₹100. Shows QR code after successful deposit request submission.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for extracting and managing URL parameters from both regular query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support for parameters like admin tokens.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom Free Fire gaming theme colors (ff-orange, ff-cyan), dark mode support, extended color palette using OKLCH color space, and custom animations for accordions.
project_state.json: Project state document describing FF PAYALUGA as a private Free Fire esports tournament platform with user registration, wallet deposits via Google Pay, tournament joins, and comprehensive admin panel for verification and management.
